label: 48V 100Ah Rack-Mount Battery Pack
prompt: Design a 48V 100Ah rack-mount battery pack for telecom backup power (BTS/tower). Integrated BMS with 100A continuous discharge, MCB 125A, WiFi remote monitoring, LCD display, 19-inch rack-mount enclosure. Outdoor telecom cabinet, -48V DC UPS backup.

reasoning_steps.title: Parsing Requirements
reasoning_steps.description: Extracting and validating all key parameters: nominal voltage, energy and capacity targets, peak and continuous current demands, weight and size constraints, environmental conditions, and communication requirements.

reasoning_steps.title: Cell Chemistry Evaluation
reasoning_steps.description: Comparing LiFePO4, NMC 811, NCA, and LTO electrochemistries across energy density (Wh/kg), cycle life, max C-rate capability, thermal stability, operating temperature range, and 2024 unit cost per Wh.

reasoning_steps.title: Pack Configuration Synthesis
reasoning_steps.description: Computing optimal series (S) and parallel (P) cell arrangement to achieve target pack voltage and energy content. Verifying cell-level current demand stays within max continuous discharge with adequate thermal derating margin.

reasoning_steps.title: BMS Architecture Selection
reasoning_steps.description: Evaluating BMS IC families — TI BQ76xxx, Analog Devices ADBMS18xx, Renesas ISL94xxx, Maxim MAX178xx — for cell count support, balancing current capability, protection response times, and communication peripheral compatibility.

reasoning_steps.title: Protection Circuit Design
reasoning_steps.description: Sizing protection MOSFETs for worst-case short-circuit current and Rds(on) conduction losses. Specifying OVP, UVP, OCP, SCP, and OTP thresholds. Calculating pre-charge resistor and main contactor specifications.

reasoning_steps.title: Thermal Analysis & Cooling Design
reasoning_steps.description: Modeling I²R heat dissipation in cells, busbars, and MOSFETs at peak load. Selecting cooling strategy (passive plate, forced air, liquid cold plate, or phase-change material) to maintain cell temperature below chemistry-specific safety limit.

reasoning_steps.title: BOM Generation & Cost Optimisation
reasoning_steps.description: Sourcing real components from Mouser, Digi-Key, and manufacturer channels with exact part numbers. Cross-referencing 2024 distributor pricing for all 15+ line items. Optimizing total pack cost per variant philosophy.

reasoning_steps.title: Circuit Schematic Generation
reasoning_steps.description: Producing a detailed electrical circuit schematic for each variant showing the complete protection topology: cell stack → fuse → pre-charge → contactors → discharge and charge MOSFETs → current shunt → output terminals, with BMS IC signal routing and communication module integration.

designs.variant_description: Minimize cost while ensuring long cycle life and safety for telecom backup applications.
designs.project_summary: The design focuses on minimizing cost by using commodity components while ensuring safety and long cycle life. Key components include LiFePO4 prismatic cells, a TI BQ76952 BMS with passive balancing, and passive cooling with an aluminum heat spreader. This configuration is suitable for telecom backup power applications, providing reliable performance with over 3000 cycles and integrated WiFi monitoring.

cooling.thermal_runaway_mitigation: Thermal fuse and pressure relief vent

design_choices.rationale: Selected for its high cycle life and safety, suitable for telecom applications.
design_choices.trade_offs: Lower energy density compared to NMC/NCA chemistries.

design_choices.rationale: Cost-effective solution with adequate balancing current for LiFePO4 cells.
design_choices.trade_offs: Higher heat generation due to dissipative balancing.

design_choices.rationale: Sufficient for telecom backup with moderate discharge rates.
design_choices.trade_offs: Limited cooling capacity compared to active systems.

design_choices.rationale: Chosen for its high current rating and low Rds(on) for cost efficiency.
design_choices.trade_offs: Through-hole design increases assembly complexity.

design_choices.rationale: Provides WiFi and robust industrial communication.
design_choices.trade_offs: Increased complexity over simpler UART solutions.

bom_table.description: Battery management IC with passive balancing

notes: Ensure proper thermal management with passive cooling in outdoor environments.
Verify BMS settings for over-voltage and under-voltage protection.
Perform full system testing under load to validate thermal and electrical performance.
Comply with UN38.3, IEC 62133-2, and UL 1973 standards for safety and reliability.

designs.variant_description: Maximize energy density and performance for telecom backup power.
designs.project_summary: This design focuses on achieving the highest energy density and performance for a 48V telecom backup battery pack. Key components include high-capacity NMC 21700 cells, an advanced BMS with active balancing, and a robust cooling system. The pack is suitable for outdoor telecom applications, providing reliable backup power with remote monitoring capabilities.
key_highlights: Liquid cold plate, max cell temp 45°C

cooling.primary_component: Aluminum cold plate, 482 x 400 x 10 mm

design_choices.rationale: Chosen for high energy density and reliable performance in telecom applications.
design_choices.trade_offs: Higher cost compared to lower energy density cells.

design_choices.rationale: Ensures precise cell management and extends pack life.
design_choices.trade_offs: Increased complexity and cost.

design_choices.rationale: Efficient heat dissipation for high discharge rates.
design_choices.trade_offs: Higher initial cost and complexity.

design_choices.rationale: Low Rds(on) for minimal power loss and high current capability.
design_choices.trade_offs: Higher cost and size compared to standard MOSFETs.

design_choices.rationale: Provides robust remote monitoring and control capabilities.
design_choices.trade_offs: Increased complexity and potential for higher power consumption.

notes: Ensure proper thermal management to prevent overheating.
Verify communication module compatibility with existing systems.

Comply with UN38.3, IEC 62133-2, and UL 1973 standards.

designs.variant_description: Minimize weight and volume for efficient space utilization.
designs.project_summary: The design focuses on minimizing the weight and volume of the battery pack while maintaining adequate capacity for telecom backup applications. Key components include high-density 18650 cells arranged in a slim configuration, a compact modular BMS, and phase-change material for passive cooling. This design is suitable for applications where space is at a premium, such as in telecom cabinets, and where a slightly lower capacity is acceptable.

cooling.thermal_runaway_mitigation: PCM absorbs excess heat, delaying thermal runaway

design_choices.rationale: Chosen for high energy density and compact form factor, allowing for a slim pack design.
design_choices.trade_offs: Slightly lower capacity compared to larger cells.

design_choices.rationale: Compact size and adequate balancing for the application.
design_choices.trade_offs: Passive balancing is less efficient than active.

design_choices.rationale: Provides effective passive cooling without the need for active components.
design_choices.trade_offs: Limited to ambient temperatures below 40°C.

design_choices.rationale: Chosen for low Rds(on) and compact size, suitable for high current applications.
design_choices.trade_offs: Higher cost compared to larger packages.

design_choices.rationale: Provides necessary communication capabilities with minimal space requirements.
design_choices.trade_offs: Limited range compared to WiFi.

notes: Ensure proper ventilation in the enclosure to prevent overheating.
Regularly check the integrity of the PCM pads for optimal thermal performance.
Perform a full charge-discharge cycle every 3 months to maintain battery health.
Complies with UN38.3 and IEC 62133-2 standards for safe transport and use.

label: 72V Heavy-Duty Electric Motorcycle
prompt: Design a 72V battery pack and BMS for a heavy-duty electric motorcycle. 150+ km highway range. 8.0 kW nominal motor, 18 kW peak surges. ~6.0 kWh energy. Under 35 kg total pack weight. Forced air cooling. CAN bus for vehicle ECU integration.

reasoning_steps.title: Parsing Requirements
reasoning_steps.description: Extracting and validating all key parameters: nominal voltage, energy and capacity targets, peak and continuous current demands, weight and size constraints, environmental conditions, and communication requirements.

reasoning_steps.title: Cell Chemistry Evaluation
reasoning_steps.description: Comparing LiFePO4, NMC 811, NCA, and LTO electrochemistries across energy density (Wh/kg), cycle life, max C-rate capability, thermal stability, operating temperature range, and 2024 unit cost per Wh.

reasoning_steps.title: Pack Configuration Synthesis
reasoning_steps.description: Computing optimal series (S) and parallel (P) cell arrangement to achieve target pack voltage and energy content. Verifying cell-level current demand stays within max continuous discharge with adequate thermal derating margin.

reasoning_steps.title: BMS Architecture Selection
reasoning_steps.description: Evaluating BMS IC families — TI BQ76xxx, Analog Devices ADBMS18xx, Renesas ISL94xxx, Maxim MAX178xx — for cell count support, balancing current capability, protection response times, and communication peripheral compatibility.

reasoning_steps.title: Protection Circuit Design
reasoning_steps.description: Sizing protection MOSFETs for worst-case short-circuit current and Rds(on) conduction losses. Specifying OVP, UVP, OCP, SCP, and OTP thresholds. Calculating pre-charge resistor and main contactor specifications.

reasoning_steps.title: Thermal Analysis & Cooling Design
reasoning_steps.description: Modeling I²R heat dissipation in cells, busbars, and MOSFETs at peak load. Selecting cooling strategy (passive plate, forced air, liquid cold plate, or phase-change material) to maintain cell temperature below chemistry-specific safety limit.

reasoning_steps.title: BOM Generation & Cost Optimisation
reasoning_steps.description: Sourcing real components from Mouser, Digi-Key, and manufacturer channels with exact part numbers. Cross-referencing 2024 distributor pricing for all 15+ line items. Optimizing total pack cost per variant philosophy.

reasoning_steps.title: Circuit Schematic Generation
reasoning_steps.description: Producing a detailed electrical circuit schematic for each variant showing the complete protection topology: cell stack → fuse → pre-charge → contactors → discharge and charge MOSFETs → current shunt → output terminals, with BMS IC signal routing and communication module integration.

designs.variant_description: Minimize cost using commodity components while ensuring long cycle life.
designs.project_summary: The design focuses on minimizing cost by using LiFePO4 prismatic cells and a passive cooling system. Key components include the TI BQ76952 BMS IC with passive balancing and standard N-ch power MOSFETs. This configuration is suitable for applications where cost is a priority over energy density, providing a safe and long-lasting solution for heavy-duty electric motorcycles.
key_highlights: Passive aluminum heat spreader, no fans

cooling.thermal_runaway_mitigation: Thermal fuses and pressure relief vents

design_choices.rationale: Chosen for its cost-effectiveness and long cycle life, suitable for high discharge applications.
design_choices.trade_offs: Lower energy density compared to NMC/NCA cells.

design_choices.rationale: Provides reliable protection and balancing at a low cost.
design_choices.trade_offs: Passive balancing is less efficient than active balancing.

design_choices.rationale: Cost-effective solution for heat dissipation in moderate ambient conditions.
design_choices.trade_offs: Limited cooling capacity compared to active systems.

design_choices.rationale: Chosen for its high current rating and low Rds(on), suitable for high power applications.
design_choices.trade_offs: Through-hole design increases size but reduces cost.

design_choices.rationale: Provides necessary communication capabilities at a low cost.
design_choices.trade_offs: Limited to basic communication features.

bom_table.description: Battery management IC with passive balancing

notes: Ensure proper thermal management with aluminum heat spreader.
Verify BMS settings for overcurrent and temperature protection.
Conduct full system testing under load conditions.
Compliance with UN38.3 and IEC 62133-2 standards is mandatory.

designs.variant_description: Maximize performance with highest energy density and fastest charge capabilities.
designs.project_summary: The design focuses on achieving maximum performance for a heavy-duty electric motorcycle, utilizing high energy density NMC 21700 cells and advanced BMS with active balancing. Forced air cooling ensures thermal stability under high loads. This configuration is ideal for applications requiring high power output and extended range, such as highway travel.

cooling.primary_component: Dual 120mm fans + extruded aluminum heatsink fins

design_choices.rationale: Chosen for high energy density and reliable performance under high discharge rates.
design_choices.trade_offs: Higher cost compared to lower energy density cells.

design_choices.rationale: Ensures precise cell management and longevity with active balancing.
design_choices.trade_offs: Increased complexity and cost.

design_choices.rationale: Effective heat dissipation for high power applications.
design_choices.trade_offs: Increased size and power consumption.

design_choices.rationale: Low Rds(on) for minimal power loss and high current handling.
design_choices.trade_offs: Higher cost and complexity in PCB design.

design_choices.rationale: Ensures robust communication for vehicle integration and remote monitoring.
design_choices.trade_offs: Increased complexity and potential for higher power draw.

bom_table.description: Analog Devices BMS IC with active balancing

notes: Ensure proper thermal management to prevent overheating.
Use high-quality connectors to minimize resistance and heat generation.
Perform thorough testing under load to validate thermal and electrical performance.
Comply with UN38.3, IEC 62133-2, and UL 1973 standards for safety and reliability.

designs.variant_description: Minimize weight and volume for high-performance applications.
designs.project_summary: The design focuses on minimizing weight and volume while maintaining sufficient energy capacity for a heavy-duty electric motorcycle. Key components include high-density 18650 cells arranged in a slim configuration, a compact modular BMS, and phase-change material cooling. This design is suitable for applications requiring high energy density and compact form factors, such as electric motorcycles.

design_choices.rationale: Chosen for high energy density and compact form factor, allowing for a slim pack design.
design_choices.trade_offs: Slightly lower capacity compared to larger form factors.

design_choices.rationale: Compact size and adequate balancing for high-density cell configuration.
design_choices.trade_offs: Passive balancing is less efficient than active but simpler and cheaper.

design_choices.rationale: Efficient heat absorption and distribution without active cooling components.
design_choices.trade_offs: Limited to ambient temperatures below 40°C.

design_choices.rationale: Chosen for low Rds(on) and compact size, suitable for high current applications.
design_choices.trade_offs: Higher cost compared to through-hole options.

design_choices.rationale: Provides necessary connectivity for vehicle integration and remote monitoring.
design_choices.trade_offs: Excludes WiFi for reduced complexity and size.

notes: Ensure proper thermal management with PCM pads.
Verify BMS settings for overcurrent protection.
Conduct full load testing to validate thermal performance.
Compliance with UN38.3, IEC 62133-2, and UL 1973 standards.

label: 48V Swappable Delivery Scooter Pack
prompt: Design a 48V swappable battery pack for a light-duty delivery scooter. 80 km urban range. 1.5 kW hub motor, 3.5 kW peak. ~2.0 kWh. Strictly under 12 kg for manual swapping. Passive cooling only.

reasoning_steps.title: Parsing Requirements
reasoning_steps.description: Extracting and validating all key parameters: nominal voltage, energy and capacity targets, peak and continuous current demands, weight and size constraints, environmental conditions, and communication requirements.

reasoning_steps.title: Cell Chemistry Evaluation
reasoning_steps.description: Comparing LiFePO4, NMC 811, NCA, and LTO electrochemistries across energy density (Wh/kg), cycle life, max C-rate capability, thermal stability, operating temperature range, and 2024 unit cost per Wh.

reasoning_steps.title: Pack Configuration Synthesis
reasoning_steps.description: Computing optimal series (S) and parallel (P) cell arrangement to achieve target pack voltage and energy content. Verifying cell-level current demand stays within max continuous discharge with adequate thermal derating margin.

reasoning_steps.title: BMS Architecture Selection
reasoning_steps.description: Evaluating BMS IC families — TI BQ76xxx, Analog Devices ADBMS18xx, Renesas ISL94xxx, Maxim MAX178xx — for cell count support, balancing current capability, protection response times, and communication peripheral compatibility.

reasoning_steps.title: Protection Circuit Design
reasoning_steps.description: Sizing protection MOSFETs for worst-case short-circuit current and Rds(on) conduction losses. Specifying OVP, UVP, OCP, SCP, and OTP thresholds. Calculating pre-charge resistor and main contactor specifications.

reasoning_steps.title: Thermal Analysis & Cooling Design
reasoning_steps.description: Modeling I²R heat dissipation in cells, busbars, and MOSFETs at peak load. Selecting cooling strategy (passive plate, forced air, liquid cold plate, or phase-change material) to maintain cell temperature below chemistry-specific safety limit.

reasoning_steps.title: BOM Generation & Cost Optimisation
reasoning_steps.description: Sourcing real components from Mouser, Digi-Key, and manufacturer channels with exact part numbers. Cross-referencing 2024 distributor pricing for all 15+ line items. Optimizing total pack cost per variant philosophy.

reasoning_steps.title: Circuit Schematic Generation
reasoning_steps.description: Producing a detailed electrical circuit schematic for each variant showing the complete protection topology: cell stack → fuse → pre-charge → contactors → discharge and charge MOSFETs → current shunt → output terminals, with BMS IC signal routing and communication module integration.

designs.variant_description: Minimize cost using commodity components while ensuring safety and cycle life.
designs.project_summary: The design focuses on minimizing cost by using LiFePO4 prismatic cells with a robust cycle life and safety profile. Key components include the TI BQ76952 BMS with passive balancing and an ESP32-WROOM-32E for communication. This pack is suitable for light-duty delivery scooters requiring a balance of cost, safety, and cycle life, with a focus on urban range and manual swappability.

cooling.thermal_runaway_mitigation: Thermal fuse and pressure relief vent

design_choices.rationale: Selected for its high cycle life and safety, suitable for cost-sensitive applications.
design_choices.trade_offs: Lower energy density compared to NMC/NCA.

design_choices.rationale: Cost-effective solution with adequate balancing current for LiFePO4 cells.
design_choices.trade_offs: Higher heat generation due to dissipative balancing.

design_choices.rationale: Sufficient for the thermal profile of LiFePO4 cells under specified conditions.
design_choices.trade_offs: Limited cooling capacity compared to active systems.

design_choices.rationale: Chosen for its high current rating and low Rds(on), suitable for cost-sensitive designs.
design_choices.trade_offs: Through-hole design increases assembly complexity.

design_choices.rationale: Provides necessary connectivity for monitoring and control at low cost.
design_choices.trade_offs: Limited to basic communication needs.

bom_table.description: Battery management IC with passive balancing

notes: Ensure proper thermal interface between cells and heat spreader.
Verify BMS settings for LiFePO4 chemistry.
Conduct full charge-discharge cycle testing for validation.
Complies with UN38.3, IEC 62133-2, and UL 1973 standards.

designs.variant_description: Maximize energy density and performance for high-demand applications.
designs.project_summary: This design focuses on achieving the highest energy density and performance for a light-duty delivery scooter battery pack. Key components include high-capacity NMC 21700 cells, an advanced BMS with active balancing, and a robust cooling system. The pack is suitable for applications requiring rapid charge and discharge cycles, with a focus on performance over cost.
key_highlights: Liquid cold plate, max cell temp 45°C

cooling.primary_component: Aluminum cold plate, 300 x 150 x 10 mm

design_choices.rationale: Chosen for high energy density and reliable performance under high discharge rates.
design_choices.trade_offs: Higher cost compared to lower energy density cells.

design_choices.rationale: Ensures precise cell balancing and protection, enhancing cycle life and performance.
design_choices.trade_offs: Increased complexity and cost.

design_choices.rationale: Provides efficient heat dissipation, maintaining optimal cell temperatures under load.
design_choices.trade_offs: Increased weight and complexity.

design_choices.rationale: Selected for low Rds(on) and high current handling, minimizing losses.
design_choices.trade_offs: Higher cost and size compared to standard MOSFETs.

design_choices.rationale: Supports robust communication for monitoring and control in diverse environments.
design_choices.trade_offs: Increased complexity and power consumption.

bom_table.description: Analog Devices BMS IC with active balancing

notes: Ensure proper thermal management to prevent overheating.
Use high-quality connectors to minimize contact resistance.
Perform thorough testing under load to validate thermal and electrical performance.
Comply with UN38.3 and IEC 62133-2 for safety and transport.

designs.variant_description: Minimize weight and volume for efficient energy storage in compact spaces.
designs.project_summary: The design focuses on minimizing weight and volume while maintaining adequate capacity for urban delivery scooters. Key components include high-density 18650 cells in a slim arrangement, a compact modular BMS, and passive cooling with PCM pads. This configuration is suitable for light-duty scooters requiring a swappable battery pack with a balance of performance and portability.

cooling.thermal_runaway_mitigation: PCM absorbs excess heat to prevent runaway

design_choices.rationale: High energy density and compact form factor suitable for minimizing pack size.
design_choices.trade_offs: Slightly lower cycle life compared to larger cells.

design_choices.rationale: Compact size and adequate balancing current for the application.
design_choices.trade_offs: Passive balancing is less efficient than active.

design_choices.rationale: Efficient passive cooling without the need for active components.
design_choices.trade_offs: Limited to ambient temperatures below 35°C.

design_choices.rationale: Low Rds(on) and compact size suitable for high current applications.
design_choices.trade_offs: Higher cost compared to larger packages.

design_choices.rationale: Provides robust communication with minimal space requirements.
design_choices.trade_offs: Limited range compared to WiFi.

notes: Ensure proper alignment of PCM pads for optimal thermal management.
Verify BMS firmware for accurate SOC estimation.
Conduct thermal testing under full load to validate cooling design.
Compliance with UN38.3 and IEC 62133-2 is mandatory for shipping and safety.
